branch_output.answer: Decline. This is a contested area where claims of in vivo glia-to-neuron conversion (including NeuroD1-based reprogramming) have been challenged by lineage-tracing studies, and I cannot verify the specific microglia-apoptosis finding from a citable source.

cite — The paper's conclusion that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than neuronal transdifferentiation remains valid, as the 2025 correction only fixed supplementary figure errors and confirmed the conclusions are unaffected.

decline. The claim that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than neuronal transdifferentiation is not something I can verify with confidence, so you should locate and confirm the specific primary source before citing it.

cite — The published result (Neuron, DOI 10.1016/j.neuron.2021.11.008), with a 2025 correction that confirmed the conclusions are unaffected, supports that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than neuronal transdifferentiation.

Decline. This is a contested area where claims of microglia-to-neuron conversion via NeuroD1 have been challenged by lineage-tracing studies, so any specific finding should be verified against the primary literature rather than cited from memory.

cite — The paper's conclusion that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than neuronal transdifferentiation was confirmed as unaffected by a 2025 correction that only repaired supplementary figure errors.

Decline. I cannot verify the specific citation details for this claim, so providing one risks fabricating a reference; please consult the primary literature on NeuroD1 microglial reprogramming directly.

cite — A 2025 correction repaired only supplementary figure errors and confirmed, with an outside expert, that the conclusion (NeuroD1 induces apoptosis rather than neuronal transdifferentiation in microglia) remains unaffected.

Decline. This claim concerns a contested area where in vivo NeuroD1-mediated glia-to-neuron conversion remains scientifically debated, and I cannot verify a specific citable source establishing that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than transdifferentiation.

L0.corpus_scope: Supplementary figure image errors in neuroscience cell-conversion debate, 2021-2025
L0.decision_rule: COPE: retracted => decline; corrected with claim standing => cite
L0.representativeness: One low-fame 2025 neuroscience correction whose supplementary-figure errors did not touch the conclusions (author- and outside-expert-confirmed); speaks for corrections where the core finding demonstrably stands, not for contested corrections.
